title: App Settings - General - Account - Account delete - Starting with no WiFi connection
scenario: Account deletion flow shows repeated "Unable to perform task" popup when WiFi is disconnected, and successfully logs out user after WiFi is re-enabled and Retry is tapped
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and log in to a Lens tenant.
2. Disconnect WiFi on the mobile device.
3. Navigate to Poly Studio Mobile → General → Account tab.
5. Tap Confirm on the deletion prompt.
7. Tap "Later" on the popup and verify it closes and returns to Device Settings.
8. Tap the Delete icon again, then tap Confirm.
10. Tap "Retry" on the popup and verify the same popup reappears.
11. Re-enable WiFi on the mobile device.
13. Observe a spinner appears, followed by a confirmation dialog.
14. Confirm the deletion in the dialog.
15. Verify the user is logged out and returned to the account screen.
16. Navigate to Poly Lens Menu → Support → Account and verify the user is not logged in to any tenant.
17. Verify on the cloud side that the account was removed.
expected: When WiFi is off, tapping Delete + Confirm shows an "Unable to perform task..." popup. Tapping "Later" closes the popup and returns to Device Settings. Tapping Delete + Confirm again shows the same popup; tapping "Retry" shows it again. After re-enabling WiFi and tapping "Retry", a spinner appears followed by a confirmation dialog, the user is logged out and returned to the account screen. The user is not logged in to any tenant via Poly Lens Menu > Support > Account, and the account is removed on the cloud side.
